examples: Statues and formulas, these mechanical tools of a serviceable use, or rather misuse, of his natural faculties, are the ankle-chains of a continuous immaturity.
The carpet is worn but still serviceable.
His Arabic was not as good as his English, but serviceable enough.
Theory must always be serviceable for policy, even if its echoes went beyond practical requirements.
The old planes were noisy and were not fuel efficient, but they were serviceable and tough.
